title: nonatelia hookeri
intro: Nonatelia hookeri, commonly known as Indian spurge or Euphorbia, is a plant species belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The therapeutic actions of Nonatelia hookeri include analgesic, anti-arthritic, and anti-cancer activities, attributed to its bioactive constituents like flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids. The bioactive constituents of Nonatelia hookeri, particularly flavonoids and terpenoids, have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.
side_effects: Nonatelia hookeri can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and headaches due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged use or excessive consumption can also lead to kidney damage, liver failure, and cardiovascular problems. In severe cases, it may cause respiratory failure, seizures, and even death. Furthermore, nonatelia hookeri can interact with other medications, exacerbating existing health conditions or causing adverse reactions.
